title: Italian Lamb Stew
description: A hearty Italian-inspired stew made with tender lamb, flavorful herbs, and a hint of sourness.

ingredients.name: Lamb, cut into 1-inch cubes
ingredients.notes: Choose a lamb with a good balance of fat and lean meat

ingredients.name: Olive oil
ingredients.notes: Use a high-quality extra-virgin olive oil

ingredients.name: Onion, diced
ingredients.notes: A sweet onion like Vidalia or Maui works well

ingredients.name: Ginger, minced
ingredients.notes: Fresh ginger adds a nice warmth to the stew

ingredients.name: Garlic, minced
ingredients.notes: Use at least 3 cloves for a strong garlic flavor

ingredients.name: Italian seasoning
ingredients.notes: A blend of herbs like thyme, rosemary, and oregano works well

ingredients.name: Sour, such as lemon juice
ingredients.notes: Use freshly squeezed lemon juice for the best flavor

ingredients.name: Mild chili flakes
ingredients.notes: Add a pinch of heat to balance out the flavors

instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the lamb and cook until browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Remove the lamb from the pot and set it aside.
Add the onion, ginger, and garlic to the pot and cook until the onion is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the Italian seasoning and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the browned lamb back to the pot, along with the sour and chili flakes.
Stir to combine and bring the stew to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 1 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Season the stew with salt and pepper to taste, and serve hot.
Serve the stew with a side of crusty bread or over mashed potatoes.
